reasoning: Both models successfully audited the PKGBUILD top-level/source-fetch gate and found it SAFE: sources come from Google's official distribution domain (edgedl.me.gvt1.com) over HTTPS with pinned SHA-256 checksums, no VCS sources, no pkgver() function, and only a harmless $CARCH conditional dependency in global scope. However, both audits were SKIPPED because `makepkg --nobuild` failed to download sources (likely because the canary build URL is version-pinned and the specific build is no longer hosted upstream). As a result, neither model reviewed the build()/package()/prepare() functions or any .install hook in their executable context. The PKGBUILD parse is safe, but the full package behavior was never assessed.
disagreement_analysis: No disagreement. Both models reached identical conclusions (PKGBUILD-safe, then skipped due to download failure).
coverage_issues: Neither model reviewed the package() or build() functions for behavior (extraction paths, symlink creation, file permissions, desktop file handling).
Any .install hook was not assessed — Android Studio packages commonly ship one to register desktop entries or set permissions.
Source download failure prevented agent-level file review, so 0 files were actually reviewed beyond the PKGBUILD parse gate.
false_negatives: Potential risks in build()/package() functions (e.g., unexpected file ops, broad chmod/chown, writing outside $pkgdir) were not evaluated because the agent never ran.
An .install script, if present, was not reviewed — this is the most security-relevant file for a desktop-app package.
re_audit_focus: Any helper scripts or .desktop launcher referenced by package()
learnings: Download failures for version-pinned canary/nightly URLs are a recurring blocker; the tool should fall back to static analysis of build()/package()/.install functions when source download fails, rather than skipping entirely.
The PKGBUILD itself can be statically reviewed for build/package/install logic even without sources — a 'sources unavailable' state should not reduce coverage to zero.
Add a dedicated check to ensure .install hooks are always enumerated and reported on, since they are high-value and independent of source availability.
Distinguish 'PKGBUILD parse gate passed' from 'full package audited' in the final verdict so that a SAFE parse gate is not mistaken for a clean full audit.
